title: Feasibility, Alternatives, Risk Experiments, and Stop Decisions · Mastery Review
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for feasibility, alternatives, risk experiments, and stop decisions and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for feasibility proof and alternative decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Inventory time, skill, access, authority, data, technology, service, policy, cost, support, and maintenance constraints with owners.
Compare build, buy, configure, integrate, simplify, manual service, policy change, partnership, and no-build alternatives.
Design the cheapest ethical experiment that distinguishes outcomes for the highest uncertainty and consequence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Constraint Inventory · changed case · Quiz Constraint Inventory
steps.instruction:
quiz case — The plan assumes unavailable data, participants, approval, services, expertise, operating budget, and time while rejecting no-build alternatives. The quiz variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Inventory time, skill, access, authority, data, technology, service, policy, cost, support, and maintenance constraints with owners.

Which action demonstrates Constraint Inventory without dropping earlier contracts?

Evidence target: Constraint Inventory · changed case · Quiz Constraint Inventory.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for inventory time, skill, access, authority, data, technology, service, policy, cost, support, and maintenance constraints with owners. For Constraint Inventory · changed case · Quiz Constraint Inventory, keep Constraint Inventory connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Stop Pivot Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Stop Pivot Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — The plan assumes unavailable data, participants, approval, services, expertise, operating budget, and time while rejecting no-build alternatives. The quiz variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Evidence target: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Stop Pivot Evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Exercise a real stop, rescope, substitute, or pivot decision when evidence crosses a predeclared threshold.
